title: Map: The plans below show a student room for two people and a student room for one pers...
description: The plans below show a student room for two people and a student room for one person at an Australian university. Summarise the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Student room (one person vs two people)
1\. Giải thích đề

Sơ đồ so sánh bố trí của phòng sinh viên dành cho một người và hai người.

→ Dạng: map / layout comparison

2\. Introduction (paraphrase)

Giữ: student room – one person – two people

Dùng:

  • shows → illustrates / compares
  • for → designed for / intended for
3\. Overview (ý chính)
  • Phòng cho 2 người rộng hơn đáng kể
  • Bố trí phức tạp hơn với nhiều nội thất hơn
  • Phòng 1 người đơn giản, tối ưu không gian cá nhân
4\. Chia BODY
BODY 1 – Phòng 2 người (chi tiết chính)
  • Kích thước: lớn hơn (6m × 4m)
  • Có 2 giường \+ 2 bàn học
  • Có thêm:
  • bàn ăn (table & chairs)
  • nhiều không gian sinh hoạt hơn
  • Bathroom: giữ ở góc trên bên trái
  • Có khu bếp (sink, stove, microwave)

→ Ý: không gian đa chức năng, dùng chung

BODY 2 – Phòng 1 người
  • Kích thước nhỏ hơn (6m × 2.5m)
  • Chỉ có:
  • 1 giường
  • 1 bàn học
  • Không có bàn ăn
  • Ít nội thất hơn → bố trí đơn giản hơn nhiều
  • Kitchen area: vẫn có nhưng gọn hơn
  • Bathroom: giữ nguyên vị trí tương tự

→ Ý: thiết kế tối giản, tập trung cá nhân

5\. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • be designed for → được thiết kế cho
  • be equipped with → được trang bị
  • be located in → nằm ở
  • be larger / smaller than → lớn hơn / nhỏ hơn
  • a shared space → không gian dùng chung
  • a compact layout → bố trí nhỏ gọn
Ví dụ
  • The larger room is designed for two occupants.
  • It is equipped with two beds and two study desks.
  • The single room has a more compact layout.
  • Fewer facilities are provided in the smaller room.
  • Both rooms include a bathroom in a similar position.

The diagrams compare the layout of a student room designed for two people and one designed for a single occupant, along with their respective weekly costs.

Overall, the room for two people is larger and contains more furniture and facilities, while the single room is more compact and simpler in design. Despite the higher total cost, the shared room appears more cost-effective per person.

The room for two people measures 6 metres by 4 metres and costs $350 per week. It includes two beds positioned on the right side, separated by a cupboard. Each occupant has a study desk, one near the top and the other near the bottom. A bathroom is located in the top-left corner, and the central area contains a table and chairs. Additional facilities such as a television, microwave, sink, and stove are arranged along the left wall.

In contrast, the single room is smaller, measuring 6 metres by 2.5 metres, and costs $200 per week. It contains only one bed and one study desk, both placed on the right side. The bathroom remains in a similar position, but the central space is more limited and does not include a table and chairs. The kitchen facilities are still present along the left wall, although arranged more compactly.
